changes_made.description: Replaced generic markdown output format with explicit JSON schemas for both task types. Added specific categorization rules (must_do_today = deadline today AND impact high).
Added stronger JSON validation instructions (close all braces). Separated prioritization vs scheduling guidance. Added overlap detection rules for scheduling.
Made JSON examples compact (single-line) to reduce truncation risk. Added WRONG/CORRECT examples for closing braces. Still failed due to write tool truncating JSON.

Key breakthrough: switched from write tool to Python script approach (json.dump). Provided complete script templates for both prioritization and scheduling tasks. Added post-write JSON validation step. This matches the pattern used by other successful qwen3-30b/openclaw skills (data-analyst, schema-markup).
Verification run with same skill. Confirmed 100/100 on both tasks.

key_findings: qwen3-30b consistently drops the final closing brace when using openclaw's write tool for JSON output
Using Python scripts with json.dump() completely solves the JSON validity issue
Providing complete script templates that the model can adapt is highly effective for smaller models
Explicit categorization rules (e.g., BOTH conditions required) prevent misclassification errors
